summary.intent: User wanted to fix a Docker environment error ("unknown option: --footer") and then commit the fix.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified as the Docker environment's fzf being too old (0.60 devel) to support --footer; entire-fzf.plugin.zsh was patched to detect --footer support at runtime via `fzf --help` and only pass the flag when supported. Change was verified (zsh -n syntax check, sourced runtime check confirming fallback branch triggers) and committed.

repo: The --footer fzf flag is used in entire-fzf.plugin.zsh's fzf invocation, which assumes a newer fzf version than what's available in the Docker image.
AGENTS.md-equivalent instructions are kept at /home/agent/.agent-stack/.codex/RTK.md and were consulted before making changes.

code.finding: Added a compatibility layer that detects --footer support once via `fzf --help` output (setting $_ENTIRE_FZF_SUPPORTS_FOOTER) and a helper function (_entire_fzf_footer_args) that conditionally includes the --footer flag, so the plugin works with both old and new fzf versions.

workflow: Verified the fix by sourcing the modified script directly in zsh and manually invoking the new detection function to confirm it resolves to the expected (unsupported) branch in this environment, in addition to a plain `zsh -n` syntax check.
Used `rtk` prefix wrapper for shell commands (rtk git, rtk zsh, rtk fzf) in this environment.

friction: Docker environment ships an older/prerelease fzf (0.60 devel) lacking --footer support, which isn't obvious until the error surfaces at runtime.
open_items: Optionally update the fzf binary bundled in the Docker image so newer fzf features can be used without compatibility shims.
